references.label: OSNI Civil Parishes — FlatGeobuf download
references.note: OSNI Baronies and Parishes maps obtained via FOI request to Mapping.Helpdesk@finance-ni.gov.uk, who commented as follows: "The Parish and Baronies dataset was created in 2007 based on the existing Townlands dataset combined with NISRA data for a specific project. While it was originally envisaged that the dataset would be released as an additional OSNI product for public supply, it proved difficult to verify the exact townlands making up parishes due to conflicts within source material. The data was developed to a state that was fit-for-purpose at the time, however, OSNI was unable to fully QA the product and so was not considered to be at a sufficient standard to add to our product catalogue. As customers enquired after the dataset, as a goodwill gesture, we agreed to share it on request, with the understanding that it is not an official product and so we could not stand over its accuracy or completeness. For the same reason, there is no documentation or analysis of the data to support it. Therefore, the only additional information we can provide you with is what we have observed from our own review of the data: there are topographical errors, such as sliver polygons, missing attribution and additional fields that could be cleansed out if desired."

maps.name: OSNI Benchmarks (Height Reference Points)
maps.description: Historic OSNI benchmarks — small brass plates or chiselled grooves set into permanent features (bridges, walls, milestones) whose heights above Ordnance Datum were precisely determined by levelling surveys. maps.name: Tellus Stream Sediments (Gold and Platinum Group Elements)

maps.name: Tellus Stream Sediments (Boron)

maps.name: Tellus Rural Soil (A Horizon — Aqua Regia Digest)

maps.name: Tellus Rural Soil (S Horizon — Aqua Regia Digest)

references.label: GSNI Tellus Rural Soil Survey — Open Data NI

maps.name: Tellus Rural Soil (S Horizon — Fire Assay for Au and PGE)
maps.description: S-horizon subsoil samples subjected to fire assay specifically to recover gold (Au) and platinum group elements. The classical mineral-prospecting technique applied across the Tellus survey grid. ~6,850 points.

maps.name: Tellus Magnetic — Total Magnetic Intensity (residual)
maps.description: Residual total magnetic intensity (TMI) from the GSNI Tellus airborne survey — variations in the Earth's magnetic field caused by buried magnetic minerals (chiefly magnetite). High-magnetic anomalies trace mafic/ultramafic intrusions, basaltic flows, and iron-bearing sedimentary basins; magnetic lows mark sedimentary basins and demagnetised alteration zones. 35m grid spacing, ±3σ rainbow ramp.

references.label: OSNI Open Data — Tellus Magnetic Grids

maps.name: Tellus Magnetic — Reduction-to-Pole (RTP)
maps.description: TMI corrected by reduction-to-pole, which compensates for the dipolar shape of magnetic anomalies caused by NI's inclined magnetic field. RTP places the peak directly above the source body, simplifying interpretation. ±3σ rainbow ramp.

references.label: OSNI Open Data — Tellus Magnetic Grids

maps.name: Tellus Magnetic — RTP Tilt Derivative
maps.description: Tilt derivative of the RTP magnetic field — an edge-detection filter that highlights the boundaries of magnetic source bodies regardless of their amplitude. Excellent for tracing dykes, faults, and contacts. ±3σ rainbow ramp.

references.label: OSNI Open Data — Tellus Magnetic Grids

maps.name: Tellus Electromagnetic — 3 kHz Apparent Conductivity
maps.description: Apparent ground conductivity at 3 kHz from the Tellus airborne EM survey — penetrates ~50–150 m, mapping conductive saline groundwater, clay-rich soils, mineralised zones, and pollution plumes. Viridis colormap (low = purple, high = yellow), -2σ to +4σ stretch.

references.label: OSNI Open Data — Tellus Electromagnetic Grids

maps.name: Tellus Electromagnetic — 14 kHz Apparent Conductivity
maps.description: Apparent ground conductivity at 14 kHz — shallower depth (~10–30 m) than the 3 kHz channel, more sensitive to surface and near-surface conductors. Useful for soil moisture, near-surface alteration, and thin clay layers. Viridis colormap, -2σ to +4σ stretch.

references.label: OSNI Open Data — Tellus Electromagnetic Grids

maps.name: Tellus Radiometric — Potassium (K %)
maps.description: Surface potassium concentration from natural gamma-ray emissions. Highlights granites and other felsic intrusions (potassium-rich), alkaline volcanics, and certain sedimentary horizons. Inferno colormap (dark = low, bright = high), ±2-3σ stretch.

references.label: OSNI Open Data — Tellus Radiometric Grids

maps.name: Tellus Radiometric — Uranium-Equivalent (eU ppm)
maps.description: Uranium-equivalent concentration from the 1.76 MeV bismuth-214 daughter peak. Highlights uranium-bearing granites, organic-rich shales, phosphatic horizons, and mineralised zones. Viridis colormap, ±2-3σ stretch.

references.label: OSNI Open Data — Tellus Radiometric Grids

maps.name: Tellus Radiometric — Thorium-Equivalent (eTh ppm)
maps.description: Thorium-equivalent concentration from the 2.61 MeV thallium-208 daughter peak. Often correlates with heavy minerals (monazite, zircon) in placer deposits and resistate sediments. Rainbow colormap, ±2-3σ stretch.

references.label: OSNI Open Data — Tellus Radiometric Grids

maps.name: Tellus Radiometric — Total Counts (gamma)
maps.description: Total gamma-ray count rate — a summed proxy for combined K, U, Th, and other radionuclide activity. Useful for identifying overall radiometric "hot" zones before decomposing into individual channels. Inferno colormap, ±2-3σ stretch.

references.label: OSNI Open Data — Tellus Radiometric Grids

maps.name: Tellus Radiometric — K/Th/U Ternary RGB
maps.description: The iconic radiometric ternary composite — K mapped to red, Th to green, U to blue. Reds = potassic (granite, certain volcanics); greens = thorium-rich (resistate sediments, heavy minerals); blues = uranium-rich (organic shales, mineralisation); whites/pale = high in all three; blacks = low overall. The most interpretively powerful single radiometric view. Each band stretched independently to mean ± 2σ.

references.label: OSNI Open Data — Tellus Radiometric Grids

maps.name: OSNI Historical Six-Inch Maps — Edition 1 (1829–1835)
maps.description: The original Six-Inch-to-One-Mile County Series — the first complete topographic survey of Ireland, produced 1829–1835 by the Ordnance Survey. 308 historical map sheets covering all six Northern Ireland counties (Antrim, Armagh, Derry/Londonderry, Down, Fermanagh, Tyrone), engraved at a scale where every individual building, road, field boundary, and town feature is shown. Mosaicked and re-tiled here at 4 m/pixel for interactive viewing on the catalogue map. For pixel-level detail, download the original sheet TIFs from the per-county packages linked below.

maps.name: Agricultural Critical Risk Areas (DAERA)
maps.description: DAERA classification of land parcels by their relative risk of contributing diffuse agricultural pollution to receiving waters. Companion to the Network Contribution datasets — the polygon equivalent of the per-river-basin SciMAP score.

references.label: OSNI Open Data — Agricultural Critical Risk Areas

maps.name: DAERA Network Contribution — SciMAP modelled pollution scores by river basin (26 catchments)
maps.description: Per-river-basin pollution-contribution scores from the SciMAP hydrological model (CEH Land Cover 2007 + Met Office rainfall + 5 m DTM), rasterised at 25 m and rendered as a Web Mercator tile pyramid coloured on inferno (low → high contribution). Raw GRIDCODE values of 58–100 stretch the inferno ramp from dark purple to pale yellow. Each parcel of land is scored for how much sediment / nutrient pollution its upstream catchment contributes to receiving waters. 26 catchment datasets were rasterised individually, mosaicked, and re-projected; per-catchment download links remain in sourceDownloads for the raw vector data.

maps.name: Areas of Outstanding Natural Beauty (AONB)
maps.description: Eight Areas of Outstanding Natural Beauty designated under Article 14 of the Nature Conservation and Amenity Lands (Northern Ireland) Order 1985 for landscape protection. Includes the Mournes, Antrim Coast & Glens, Causeway Coast, Ring of Gullion, Sperrins, Strangford Lough, Erne Lakeland and Lagan Valley AONBs.

references.label: Open Data NI — Areas of Outstanding Natural Beauty (AONB)

maps.name: Areas of Special Scientific Interest (ASSI)
maps.description: Areas of Special Scientific Interest provide statutory protection under the Nature Conservation and Amenity Lands (NI) Order 1985 for the best examples of NI's flora, fauna, geological or physiographical features. The 394 ASSIs are NI's principal site-based wildlife protection mechanism.

references.label: Open Data NI — Areas of Special Scientific Interest (ASSI)

maps.name: National Nature Reserves (NNR)
maps.description: Statutory nature reserves declared under the Nature Conservation and Amenity Lands (NI) Order 1985 for areas of importance for flora, fauna, geological or other special features. The 50 NNRs are managed primarily for conservation, with the highest level of legal site protection in NI.

references.label: Open Data NI — National Nature Reserves (NNR)

maps.name: Ramsar Wetland Sites
maps.description: Wetlands of international importance designated under the 1971 Ramsar Convention on Wetlands. The 20 NI Ramsar sites cover loughs, estuaries and bogs of internationally significant ecological character.

references.label: Open Data NI — Ramsar Wetland Sites

maps.name: Special Areas of Conservation (SAC)
maps.description: SACs are habitats and species given the greatest protection under the EU Habitats Directive (transposed into UK law via the Conservation Regulations). 58 sites in NI form part of the Natura 2000 network — the highest level of habitat protection in European law.

references.label: Open Data NI — Special Areas of Conservation (SAC)

maps.name: Special Protection Areas (SPA)
maps.description: Designated under the EU Birds Directive for vulnerable, migratory and other significant bird species. 16 SPAs in NI form part of the Natura 2000 network. Many overlap with Ramsar / SAC / ASSI designations.

references.label: Open Data NI — Special Protection Areas (SPA)

maps.name: World Heritage Site
maps.description: Northern Ireland's sole UNESCO World Heritage Site: the Giant's Causeway and Causeway Coast. Inscribed in 1986 under criteria (vii) and (viii) for its outstanding geological and natural features — the famous columnar basalt formations and associated coastal landscape.

references.label: Open Data NI — World Heritage Site

maps.name: Landscape Character Areas (LCA)
maps.description: The Northern Ireland Landscape Character Assessment (NIEA, 2000) subdivides the countryside into 130 Landscape Character Areas based on local geology, landform, land use, settlement and ecology. LCAs underpin landscape policy and planning decisions.

references.label: Open Data NI — Landscape Character Areas (LCA)
